THIS LSR REFLECTS ONE COUNTY SEGMENT OF A TORNADO WHICH PASSED THROUGH PARTS OF PERRY COUNTY... RANDOLPH COUNTY... AND JACKSON COUNTY. PERRY AND JACKSON COUNTIES ARE COVERED BY WFO PAH. RANDOLPH COUNTY IS COVERED BY WFO LSX. THIS LSR COVERS THE RANDOLPH COUNTY SEGMENT. DAMAGE WAS CONSISTENT WITH AN EF1 TORNADO. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 3.1 MILES ACROSS SOUTHERN RANDOLPH COUNTY BUT THE TOTAL PATH LENGTH ACROSS ALL COUNTIES WAS STILL UNKNOWN. THE PATH WIDTH IN RANDOLPH COUNTY WAS 700 YARDS.

RANDOLPH COUNTY IS COVERED BY WFO LSX. THIS LSR COVERS THE RANDOLPH COUNTY SEGMENT. DAMAGE WAS CONSISTENT WITH AN EF2 TORNADO. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 3.1 MILES ACROSS SOUTHERN RANDOLPH COUNTY BUT THE TOTAL PATH LENGTH ACROSS ALL COUNTIES WAS STILL UNKNOWN. THE PATH WIDTH IN RANDOLPH COUNTY WAS 700 YARDS.

A NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED A EF-1 TORNADO BEGAN AROUND 13.4 MILES SOUTHWEST OF SHELBINA AND MOVED NORTHEAST LIFTING 5.5 MILES NORTHEAST OF SHELBINA. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 16 MILES WITH A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 150 YARDS.

*** 1 INJ *** EF-1 TORNADO WAS SURVEYED WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 100 MPH. THE TORNADO WAS 16 MILES LONG AND 150 YARDS WIDE. THE BULK OF THE DAMAGE WAS EF-0...PRIMARILY TO TREES AND FARM BUILDINGS. ONE HOME TO THE NORTHEAST OF THE HIGH SCHOOL HAD A PORTION OF ITS ROOF BLOWN OFF. ONE MINOR INJURY OCCURRED WHEN A MAN WAS CUT IN THE FOREHEAD BY FLYING DEBRIS.

A NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED AN EF-1 TORNADO BEGAN AROUND 0.75 MILES WEST NORTHWEST OF ARGYLE AND MOVED EAST NORTHEAST ACROSS SOUTHERN OSAGE COUNTY LIFTING 1 MILE NORTHEAST OF FREEBURG. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 7.3 MILES WITH A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 75 YARDS.

DELAYED REPORT. WINDS OVERTURNED A 16 FOOT DIAMETER POOL WHICH HAD UP TO 1 FOOT OF WATER IN IT. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

A NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED AN EF-1 TORNADO BEGAN IN RHINELAND AND MOVED EAST-NORTHEAST ACROSS SOUTHWEST WARREN COUNTY LIFTING AROUND 2 MILES SSE OF WARRENTON. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 21 MILES WITH A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 150 YARDS.

EF1 TORNADO SURVEYED WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 100 MPH. THE TORNADO WAS 21 MILES LONG AND 150 YARDS WIDE. THE TORNADO DID MOSTLY TREE DAMAGE....BUT DID ALSO DO SOME STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO HOMESTEADS AND FARM BUILDINGS FROM RHINELAND MISSOURI TO JUST SOUTH OF WARRENTON MISSOURI.
UTILITY POLE BLOWN OVER

A NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED AN EF-1 TORNADO BEGAN ON THE WEST SIDE OF SAWYERVILLE AND MOVED NORTHEAST ACROSS SOUTHWEST MACOUPIN COUNTY INTO WESTERN MONTGOMERY COUNTY LIFTING AROND 2.7 MILES SOUTHEAST OF LITCHFIELD. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 13 MILES WITH A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 100 YARDS.

*** 2 INJ *** FIVE MOBILE HOMES BLOWN OVER... ANOTHER ONE HAD A TREE FALL ON IT IN WENTZVILLE NEAR WEST MAIN STREET AND HILLTOP DRIVE. TWO PEOPLE TRAPPED AT THIS TIME. GAS LEAK IN AREA. TWO MINOR INJURIES.
DEBRIS ON BOTH SIDES OF I-70 NEAR CHURCH STREET/HWY Z EXIT. TRACTOR TRAILER BLOW OVER IN PARKING LOT OF SUPER 8 MOTEL. STRUCTURAL COLLAPSE OF WENTZVILLE MILLS WORKS. NO INJURIES REPORTED.
MOBILE HOME BLOWN OVER IN WENTZVILLE.
TWO MOBILE HOMES BLOWN OVER IN WENTZVILLE NEAR WEST MAIN STREET AND HILLTOP DRIVE. TWO PEOPLE TRAPPED AT THIS TIME. GAS LEAK IN AREA.
SEVERAL BUSINESSES SUSTAINED MINOR DAMAGE ON SOUTH END OF WENTZVILLE PARKWAY...SOUTH OF I-70.

*** 3 INJ *** EF-1 TORNADO WAS SURVEYED WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 100 MPH. THE TORNADO WAS 3 MILES LONG AND 100 YARDS WIDE. THIS TORNADO DID DAMAGE TO A NUMBER OF HOMES AND BUSINESSES ALONG INTERSTATE 70...WHICH IT CROSSED 3 TIMES. THE TORNADO THEN CONTINUED INTO A MOBILE HOME PARK ON THE WEST SIDE OF WENTZVILLE WHERE THE 3 INJURIES OCCURRED.

*** 3 INJ *** A NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED AN EF-1 TORNADO BEGAN 3 MILES WEST OF WENTZVILLE AND MOVED EAST-NORTHEAST INTO DOWNTOWN WENTZVILLE. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 3 MILES WITH A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 100 YARDS.

*** 3 INJ *** FIVE MOBILE HOMES BLOWN OVER... ANOTHER ONE HAD A TREE FALL ON IT IN WENTZVILLE NEAR WEST MAIN STREET AND HILLTOP DRIVE. TWO PEOPLE TRAPPED AT THIS TIME. GAS LEAK IN AREA. THREE MINOR INJURIES.

A WEAK TORNADO MOVED ACROSS FAR NORTHERN ST. CHARLES COUNTY CAUSING EF0 DAMAGE TO TREES, OUTBUILDINGS, HOMES, BOATS, CAMPERS AND A FEW BOAT DOCKS. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED TO BE 80 MPH AND THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR NEARLY 4 MILES AND HAD A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 100 YARDS.

THE NWS CONDUCTED A DAMAGE SURVEY OF THE PIASA MARINA WHERE DAMAGE OCCURRED TO SEVERAL BOAT DOCKS, AN UNOCCUPIED BUILDING, AND SOME SURROUNDING TREES. THE SURVEY CONCLUDED THIS DAMAGE WAS THE RESULT OF A MICROBURST WITH WINDS ESTIMATED AT 80-90 MPH.

A WEAK TORNADO FORMED ONE MILE EAST OF WILLIAMSBURG, MISSOURIALONG OLD US 40 AT A RESIDENCE AND BUSINESS WHERE TWO BARNS WERE DAMAGED AND ANOTHER WAS DESTROYED. TWO VEHICLES WERE ALSO FLIPPED AT THIS LOCATION. DEBRIS WAS TOSSED UP TO MILE DOWN THE TORNADOES PATH INTO THE ADJACENT FARM FIELD AND TREE LINE. THE DAMAGE WAS RATED EF1 WITH WINDS ESTIMATED AT 100 MPH. THE TORNADO CROSSED COUNTY ROAD 1031 SNAPPING A POWER POLE. THE TORNADO DISSIPATED SHORTLY AFTER CROSSING COUNTY ROAD 1033.
LARGE TREE BRANCH BLOWN DOWN AND PEA SIZE HAIL.
SEVERAL TREES SNAPPED AT THE BASE. POWER LINES AND NUMEROUS TREE LIMBS DOWN.

A WEAK TORNADO FORMED ON THE SOUTH OUTER ROAD OF INTERSTATE 70 ONE MILE SOUTHEAST OF NEW FLORENCE. IT CAUSED DAMAGE TO TWO BUILDINGS AT A MODOT FACILITY, INCLUDING THE REMOVAL OF 12 GARAGE DOORS. THE TORNADO TOSSED THESE DOORS AND ROOFING MATERIAL INTO THE POWER LINES, CAUSING ONE OF THE POLES TO SNAP. SOME OF THE MATERIAL LANDED ACROSS INTERSTATE 70 AT A RESIDENCE ON MO WW. THERE WAS MINOR DAMAGE TO THE HOUSE WITH A GARAGE DOOR RUINED AND THE PORCH PILLARS SHIFTED OFF THE FOUNDATION. DEBRIS STRETCHED INTO THE ADJACENT FIELD. THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST AND CAUSED ROOF AND DOOR DAMAGE TO THREE BARNS ON HUDSON ROAD EAST OF NEW FLORENCE. THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST AND ENDED SHORTLY AFTER DESTROYING A BARN ON COUNTY ROAD 230, SOUTH OF MANLEY ROAD. FURTHER EAST ALONG INTERSTATE 70, NEAR MILE MARKER 177, A BROAD SWATH OF STRAIGHT LINE WINDS (NEARLY A MILE WIDE) SNAPPED SIX POWER POLES AND FLIPPED A SEMI ON ITS SIDE.
